title: GameWorld: Towards Standardized and Verifiable Evaluation of Multimodal Game Agents

details.title: GameWorld: Towards Standardized and Verifiable Evaluation of Multimodal Game Agents
details.abstract: Proposes GameWorld, a standardized, verifiable benchmark for evaluating multimodal game agents across 34 browser games and 170 tasks using semantic actions and keyboard/mouse interfaces.

title: OccuBench: Evaluating AI Agents on Real-World Professional Tasks via Language World Models

details.title: OccuBench: Evaluating AI Agents on Real-World Professional Tasks via Language World Models
details.abstract: AI agents excel at coding and web tasks, but most real-world professional scenarios lack executable environments. OccuBench uses LLM-based Language World Models to simulate 100 professional task environments (emergency triage, customs processing, etc.) and evaluates 15 frontier models on both task completion and robustness under environmental faults. Key findings: (1) no single model dominates all industries; (2) implicit faults are harder than explicit ones; (3) scaling and stronger reasoning still help; (4) strong agents aren't necessarily strong simulators.

title: TREX: Automating LLM Fine-tuning via Agent-Driven Tree-based Exploration

details.title: TREX: Automating LLM Fine-tuning via Agent-Driven Tree-based Exploration
details.abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have empowered AI research agents to perform isolated scientific tasks, automating complex, real-world workflows, such as LLM training, remains a significant challenge. In this paper, we introduce TREX, a multi-agent system that automates the entire LLM training life-cycle. By orchestrating collaboration between two core modules-the Researcher and the Executor-the system seamlessly performs requirement analysis, open-domain literature and data research, formulation of training strategies, preparation of data recipes, and model training and evaluation. The multi-round experimental process is modeled as a search tree, enabling the system to efficiently plan exploration paths, reuse historical results, and distill high-level insights from iterative trials. To evaluate the capability of automated LLM training, we construct FT-Bench, a benchmark comprising 10 tasks derived from real-world scenarios, ranging from optimizing fundamental model capabilities to enhancing performance on domain-specific tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that the TREX agent consistently optimizes model performance on target tasks.

title: TIP: Token Importance in On-Policy Distillation

details.title: TIP: Token Importance in On-Policy Distillation
details.abstract: Useful learning signal in OPD concentrates in uncertain tokens and overconfident mistakes, and selecting tokens based on entropy + divergence enables more efficient training with far fewer tokens.

title: UI-Copilot: Advancing Long-Horizon GUI Automation via Tool-Integrated Policy Optimization

details.title: UI-Copilot: Advancing Long-Horizon GUI Automation via Tool-Integrated Policy Optimization
details.abstract: We propose UI-Copilot, a collaborative framework where the GUI agent selectively invokes a lightweight copilot for memory retrieval and numerical computation, enabling efficient long-horizon GUI navigation.
